.ddx/attachments/ddx-ee2cd434/events.jsonl:12:{"actor":"ddx","body":"no_changes_rationale — bead ddx-ee2cd434 (SD-024 Stage 2)

(a) WHAT IS DONE
----------------
The previous attempt (commit 5ae02964) migrated the high-value `r.Run(RunOptions{...})`
and `RunViaService(..., RunOptions{...})` test sites onto the SD-024 successor API
(`r.RunWithConfig` + `RunWithConfigViaService` + `AgentRunRuntime`) across:

  - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go    (~22 sites: TestRunWith*, TestSessionLogging,
                                         TestRunPromptFile, TestRunStdinMode,
                                         TestRunModel*, TestRunNonZeroExit,
                                         TestRunUnknownHarness, TestRunEmptyPrompt,
                                         TestIntegration_CodexEcho, TestRunOpencode*,
                                         and the *Permissions* pair)
  - cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go   (lmstudio / openrouter dispatch regressions)
  - cli/internal/agent/script_test.go   (runScript helper now builds rcfg via
                                         TestRunConfigOpts and dispatches via
                                         RunWithConfigViaService)

Those 26 sites are the bulk of the "test sites that exercise the high-level agent
run path" called out in the bead description.

(b) WHAT IS BLOCKING
--------------------
The bead AC reads:

    rg 'RunOptions\{|CompareOptions\{|QuorumOptions\{' cli/ returns 0 matches outside
    the type definitions about to be retired.

This is a *global* gate. It cannot be satisfied by test-only changes — it requires
retiring every remaining `RunOptions{...}` construction in both production and test
code. The remaining sites fall into two classes:

CLASS A — tests that call functions whose signature *takes* RunOptions, so the
RunOptions construction is not a "test site" the test could rewrite without
changing the function under test:

  - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go        BuildArgs(h, RunOptions{...}, model)  — 14 sites
                                            BuildArgs is the function under test.
  - cli/internal/agent/prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go
        line 78  resolvePrompt(RunOptions{PromptFile})              — exercises r.resolvePrompt
        line 85  defaultResolvePromptForCompare(RunOptions{...})    — exercises pkg-level helper
        line 112 RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})  — exercises stub-injection helper
  - cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go
        line 195 SessionIndexEntryFromResult(root, RunOptions{...}, ...)
        line 278 RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/models_test.go
        line 45  BenchmarkArmsToCompare(arms, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/virtual_test.go
        lines 164/174/214 runVirtualFn(runner, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/claude_stream_test.go
        lines 206/212/218/224/291/324  internal opts: RunOptions{...} fixtures

Migrating any of these test sites requires first changing the *signature* of the
production function under test (BuildArgs, resolvePrompt, defaultResolvePromptForCompare,
RunViaServiceWith, runVirtualFn, BenchmarkArmsToCompare, SessionIndexEntryFromResult,
plus the internal claude_stream helpers). That is production-code refactor work,
explicitly outside the scope the bead description names.

CLASS B — production callers still constructing RunOptions{...}:

  - cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go              :1462
  - cli/internal/server/server.go     :1913, :3786
  - cli/internal/exec/store.go        :201
  - cli/internal/agent/runner.go      :248, :281, :300, :321, :976
  - cli/internal/agent/service_run.go :82  (RunWithConfigViaService bridge — by design)
  - cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go :61, :261, :414, :685
  - cli/internal/agent/grade.go       :169
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go :706
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go :531

Until these are migrated (and in many cases until the function signatures they
flow through are migrated), the AC's grep gate cannot return zero matches.

The fresh-eyes review on the prior attempt itself concluded:

    Path forward: simplest fix is splitting follow-up beads for
    (a) prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go,
    (b) remaining *_test.go RunOptions sites,
    (c) production-code migration,
    then closing this bead with a narrower AC.

That assessment is correct. The bead title ("5-10 sites") and description ("migrate
test sites … in agent_test.go and any sibling test using RunOptions") describe a
test-scoped refactor; the AC describes a global retirement. The two are not
compatible inside one bead.

(c) WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT WOULD NEED
---------------------------------------
Either:

  Option 1 (preferred): re-cut this bead with a narrowed AC, e.g.

      "rg 'RunOptions\{' cli/internal/agent/{agent,runner,script}_test.go returns
      0 matches in tests that exercise r.Run / RunViaService."

      That AC matches the test-only scope, and the prior commit already satisfies it.

  Option 2: expand bead scope to cover the production migration as well. That is
      a multi-file Stage-2 refactor touching cmd/, internal/server/, internal/exec/,
      and most of internal/agent/. It also requires migrating the signatures of
      BuildArgs, resolvePrompt, defaultResolvePromptForCompare, RunViaServiceWith,
      runVirtualFn, BenchmarkArmsToCompare, and SessionIndexEntryFromResult so the
      test sites in Class A can shed RunOptions. This belongs in its own bead (or
      epic), not in a "5-10 sites" test-migration bead.

No new code is committed in this attempt because:

  1. The work the bead description actually scopes is already done (5ae02964).
  2. Doing test-only edits that don't move the AC gate would be exploratory churn.
  3. Doing the full production migration here would silently expand the bead scope
     past what the description and title authorize.

  :520): on graph build, cap prompt-metadata string lengths the same
  way; oversize values fail the graph build with an actionable error.

- Agent-side file readers — Stage D1.
- Server text egress — Stage E1/E2.
- Any client-side UI for the new 413 response code — separate work.

.ddx/executions/20260504T002741-afe6c04c/decisions.md:3:Backfill of production-reachability violations in package `internal/agent/testfixtures`.
.ddx/executions/20260504T002741-afe6c04c/decisions.md:5:The bead listed 6 unreached symbols; deadcode RTA actually flagged 7 (the 6 listed `ReviewFailureRunner` methods plus the local `reviewerFunc.ReviewBead` adapter). All live in a single file (`cli/internal/agent/testfixtures/review_failure_runner.go`) consumed only by `_test.go` files in three other packages (`cmd`, `internal/server`, `internal/server/graphql`) plus the package's own internal tests. Production code never imports `testfixtures`, so no path from `main()` reaches any of these symbols and `deadcode` flags every exported entry as dead.
.ddx/executions/20260504T002741-afe6c04c/decisions.md:10:2. The `// wiring:pending <bead-id>` annotation path is incompatible with AC #3, which requires the bare `deadcode` CLI (which does not honor the annotation) to report zero remaining symbols in the package.
.ddx/executions/20260504T002741-afe6c04c/decisions.md:12:Therefore the entire `testfixtures` package is DELETED and each of the three external test consumers receives a minimal test-local copy of the fixture (`reviewFailureRunner` + `reviewerFn` adapter). This matches the precedent set by ddx-83b8994f (bbe9fe2e — "Tests that exercised dead helpers either deleted or rewired to test-local adapters") and ddx-abb40ce5 (2224f93c — DELETE preferred when AC #3 requires zero deadcode reports).

.ddx/executions/20260504T022245-36a2ca7b/prompt.md:164:+    "description": "The bug at ddx-29058e2a (5 CLI flags silently dropped on the server path) is a symptom of a deeper architectural issue: the ExecuteLoopSpec is defined as parallel structs that must be hand-maintained in lockstep. Adding any flag requires editing 5+ places, and forgetting one is silent.

CURRENT STATE (the smell)

Each flag traverses 6 boundaries:

1. cobra extract: cmd.Flags().GetX("flag") → local var (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1573-1611)
2. positional param: passed to executeLoopWithServer / runExecuteLoopLocal (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1627, ~1941 signature)
3. loose JSON map: workerSpec map[string]interface{} (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:~1948)
4. server request struct: hand-maintained in handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (cli/internal/server/server.go:2246-2260)
5. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal: server.go:2289-2303 → cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49
6. runWorker per-field consumption: cli/internal/server/workers.go ~656

ALSO IN SCOPE — the GraphQL parallel path (per codex review):
- workerDispatchAdapter at cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:62-75, 123-137 has its own request struct AND its own StartExecuteLoop literal — same parallel-struct smell on a different transport. Unification must DELETE this too, otherwise the refactor fixes REST and leaves GraphQL re-broken.

Two of those (steps 4 and 5) are typed but maintained in parallel. The 5 dropped flags (opaque_passthrough, max_cost, request_timeout, min_power, max_power) fell out exactly there. PLUS FromRev is also dropped on the server path (cobra flag at agent_cmd.go:1560 + work.go:58, consumed locally at agent_cmd.go:1753 ExecuteBeadRuntime{FromRev: fromRev}, never reaches the server). The bug fix would be local — add fields in 5 places — but the ROOT CAUSE recurs the next time a flag is added.

DESIGN — single canonical ExecuteLoopSpec struct

Define one struct, used at every layer:

  cobra RunE → parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions)
                ↓
       (--local) runExecuteLoopInline(spec)
                ↓
       (default) httpSubmitExecuteLoop(spec)  ── JSON ──▶ server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker
                                                   ↓ (decode INTO THE SAME struct, then Validate + ApplyDefaults)
                                                startExecuteLoop(spec)
                                                   ↓ (persist as spec.json — SAME struct, fully-resolved)
                                                runWorker(spec)

Properties:
- One struct definition. JSON tags drive both wire format and disk persistence.
- Cobra binding is the only per-flag code (irreducible — cobra needs to know each flag).
- --local and server paths consume the same struct; behavior parity is structural, not asserted by tests.
- Adding a flag = (a) one struct field + (b) one cobra binding. Drop is impossible by construction — the JSON tag round-trips through wire and persistence.
- DispatchOptions is a SEPARATE small struct holding control-plane flags (--local, --json) that are NOT execution-spec fields and must NOT be persisted on disk.

CRITICAL CONTRACTS (per codex review)

1. Wire format for time.Duration: Go's default time.Duration JSON marshaling produces nanoseconds, but current code sends poll_interval as a string (agent_cmd.go:1969-1971). The unified spec MUST commit to one format — either custom Duration type with string MarshalJSON, or canonical numeric. Pick string (operator-readable spec.json wins) and add UnmarshalJSON tolerating both.

2. Validate + ApplyDefaults lifecycle:
   - parseExecuteLoopSpec returns spec + DispatchOptions, with cobra defaults applied
   - Spec.Validate() is called BEFORE HTTP submit (client-side guard)
   - server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes → calls Spec.ApplyDefaults() → Spec.Validate() BEFORE StartExecuteLoop (server cannot trust the wire)
   - GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter goes through THE SAME StartExecuteLoop entry point so Validate + ApplyDefaults are guaranteed
   - spec.json on disk is fully-resolved (no implicit defaults at runWorker time)

3. Forward-compat stance: ddx is single-repo, single-version client/server today. Add SpecVersion int field; server returns 400 with capability list if SpecVersion > supported. Newer-server-older-client: existing fields unchanged, new fields ignored on both sides (the current default). Make this explicit in the AC; do not rely on json's silent unknown-field tolerance as the only contract.

4. Backward-compat for old persisted specs: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ have legacy fields (e.g., min_tier/max_tier in older specs at .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T083934-f77c/spec.json:5-6). UnmarshalJSON must tolerate unknown fields when LOADING from disk (json.Decoder default behavior is fine here; do NOT enable DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads).

LOCATION

Likely cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go (a small focused package). Importable from both cli/cmd/ and cli/internal/server/ without import cycles. NOTE: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744) needs to know about this new package as an entry-root for the production-reachability checker.

NOT IN SCOPE

- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, ddx run, MCP agent dispatch, ddx work passthrough variants) — same smell may exist; covered by the sister audit bead ddx-fb290074.
- Reworking client→server transport (HTTP/JSON stays as-is).
- Changing validation logic itself — only ensuring it lives on the spec so both paths apply it.
- Renaming flags or reshaping CLI surface.
- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string — that is a picker semantics change, owned by ddx-9d55601f. Keep LabelFilter as string to keep ddx-9d55601f landing in parallel.

USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM (originally what filed this bead)
- ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server.
- Root cause: opaque_passthrough was dropped at the server's request struct. With OpaquePassthrough lost, the server-spawned worker validates the harness against its provider list (which rejects 'claude') instead of treating it as opaque-passthrough.
- The unification refactor solves this by construction — there's no second struct to omit OpaquePassthrough from. NOTE: OpaquePassthrough has TWO skip points to verify (cli/internal/server/workers.go:314-317 AND :752-773), not one.

ABSORBS THE NARROW FIX
The 5 originally-listed dropped flags + FromRev all become fields on the unified spec. They cannot be dropped after this refactor. The refactor IS the fix.

SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS

- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): touches execute_bead_loop.go's Run loop. Should land BEFORE this OR be tightly coordinated. If unification lands first, dc157075 redoes defaulting + persistence. Recommend dc157075 first.
- ddx-9d55601f (picker priority bug): does NOT block on this. Keep LabelFilter type stable. Picker fix lands in parallel.
- ddx-a946c744 (REACH-PROTO): needs to know about new cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ package as entry root.",

.ddx/executions/20260504T021759-c712d0e8/prompt.md:5:The bug at ddx-29058e2a (5 CLI flags silently dropped on the server path) is a symptom of a deeper architectural issue: the ExecuteLoopSpec is defined as parallel structs that must be hand-maintained in lockstep. Adding any flag requires editing 5+ places, and forgetting one is silent.

CURRENT STATE (the smell)

Each flag traverses 6 boundaries:

1. cobra extract: cmd.Flags().GetX("flag") → local var (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1573-1611)
2. positional param: passed to executeLoopWithServer / runExecuteLoopLocal (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1627, ~1941 signature)
3. loose JSON map: workerSpec map[string]interface{} (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:~1948)
4. server request struct: hand-maintained in handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (cli/internal/server/server.go:2246-2260)
5. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal: server.go:2289-2303 → cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49
6. runWorker per-field consumption: cli/internal/server/workers.go ~656

ALSO IN SCOPE — the GraphQL parallel path (per codex review):
- workerDispatchAdapter at cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:62-75, 123-137 has its own request struct AND its own StartExecuteLoop literal — same parallel-struct smell on a different transport. Unification must DELETE this too, otherwise the refactor fixes REST and leaves GraphQL re-broken.

Two of those (steps 4 and 5) are typed but maintained in parallel. The 5 dropped flags (opaque_passthrough, max_cost, request_timeout, min_power, max_power) fell out exactly there. PLUS FromRev is also dropped on the server path (cobra flag at agent_cmd.go:1560 + work.go:58, consumed locally at agent_cmd.go:1753 ExecuteBeadRuntime{FromRev: fromRev}, never reaches the server). The bug fix would be local — add fields in 5 places — but the ROOT CAUSE recurs the next time a flag is added.

DESIGN — single canonical ExecuteLoopSpec struct

Define one struct, used at every layer:

  cobra RunE → parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions)
                ↓
       (--local) runExecuteLoopInline(spec)
                ↓
       (default) httpSubmitExecuteLoop(spec)  ── JSON ──▶ server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker
                                                   ↓ (decode INTO THE SAME struct, then Validate + ApplyDefaults)
                                                startExecuteLoop(spec)
                                                   ↓ (persist as spec.json — SAME struct, fully-resolved)
                                                runWorker(spec)

Properties:
- One struct definition. JSON tags drive both wire format and disk persistence.
- Cobra binding is the only per-flag code (irreducible — cobra needs to know each flag).
- --local and server paths consume the same struct; behavior parity is structural, not asserted by tests.
- Adding a flag = (a) one struct field + (b) one cobra binding. Drop is impossible by construction — the JSON tag round-trips through wire and persistence.
- DispatchOptions is a SEPARATE small struct holding control-plane flags (--local, --json) that are NOT execution-spec fields and must NOT be persisted on disk.

CRITICAL CONTRACTS (per codex review)

1. Wire format for time.Duration: Go's default time.Duration JSON marshaling produces nanoseconds, but current code sends poll_interval as a string (agent_cmd.go:1969-1971). The unified spec MUST commit to one format — either custom Duration type with string MarshalJSON, or canonical numeric. Pick string (operator-readable spec.json wins) and add UnmarshalJSON tolerating both.

2. Validate + ApplyDefaults lifecycle:
   - parseExecuteLoopSpec returns spec + DispatchOptions, with cobra defaults applied
   - Spec.Validate() is called BEFORE HTTP submit (client-side guard)
   - server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes → calls Spec.ApplyDefaults() → Spec.Validate() BEFORE StartExecuteLoop (server cannot trust the wire)
   - GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter goes through THE SAME StartExecuteLoop entry point so Validate + ApplyDefaults are guaranteed
   - spec.json on disk is fully-resolved (no implicit defaults at runWorker time)

3. Forward-compat stance: ddx is single-repo, single-version client/server today. Add SpecVersion int field; server returns 400 with capability list if SpecVersion > supported. Newer-server-older-client: existing fields unchanged, new fields ignored on both sides (the current default). Make this explicit in the AC; do not rely on json's silent unknown-field tolerance as the only contract.

4. Backward-compat for old persisted specs: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ have legacy fields (e.g., min_tier/max_tier in older specs at .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T083934-f77c/spec.json:5-6). UnmarshalJSON must tolerate unknown fields when LOADING from disk (json.Decoder default behavior is fine here; do NOT enable DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads).

LOCATION

Likely cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go (a small focused package). Importable from both cli/cmd/ and cli/internal/server/ without import cycles. NOTE: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744) needs to know about this new package as an entry-root for the production-reachability checker.

NOT IN SCOPE

- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, ddx run, MCP agent dispatch, ddx work passthrough variants) — same smell may exist; covered by the sister audit bead ddx-fb290074.
- Reworking client→server transport (HTTP/JSON stays as-is).
- Changing validation logic itself — only ensuring it lives on the spec so both paths apply it.
- Renaming flags or reshaping CLI surface.
- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string — that is a picker semantics change, owned by ddx-9d55601f. Keep LabelFilter as string to keep ddx-9d55601f landing in parallel.

USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM (originally what filed this bead)
- ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server.
- Root cause: opaque_passthrough was dropped at the server's request struct. With OpaquePassthrough lost, the server-spawned worker validates the harness against its provider list (which rejects 'claude') instead of treating it as opaque-passthrough.
- The unification refactor solves this by construction — there's no second struct to omit OpaquePassthrough from. NOTE: OpaquePassthrough has TWO skip points to verify (cli/internal/server/workers.go:314-317 AND :752-773), not one.

ABSORBS THE NARROW FIX
The 5 originally-listed dropped flags + FromRev all become fields on the unified spec. They cannot be dropped after this refactor. The refactor IS the fix.

SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS

- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): touches execute_bead_loop.go's Run loop. Should land BEFORE this OR be tightly coordinated. If unification lands first, dc157075 redoes defaulting + persistence. Recommend dc157075 first.
- ddx-9d55601f (picker priority bug): does NOT block on this. Keep LabelFilter type stable. Picker fix lands in parallel.
- ddx-a946c744 (REACH-PROTO): needs to know about new cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ package as entry root.

5. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal: server.go:2289-2303 → cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49
6. runWorker per-field consumption: cli/internal/server/workers.go ~656

ALSO IN SCOPE — the GraphQL parallel path (per codex review):
- workerDispatchAdapter at cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:62-75, 123-137 has its own request struct AND its own StartExecuteLoop literal — same parallel-struct smell on a different transport. Unification must DELETE this too, otherwise the refactor fixes REST and leaves GraphQL re-broken.

Two of those (steps 4 and 5) are typed but maintained in parallel. The 5 dropped flags (opaque_passthrough, max_cost, request_timeout, min_power, max_power) fell out exactly there. PLUS FromRev is also dropped on the server path (cobra flag at agent_cmd.go:1560 + work.go:58, consumed locally at agent_cmd.go:1753 ExecuteBeadRuntime{FromRev: fromRev}, never reaches the server). The bug fix would be local — add fields in 5 places — but the ROOT CAUSE recurs the next time a flag is added.

DESIGN — single canonical ExecuteLoopSpec struct

Define one struct, used at every layer:

  cobra RunE → parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions)
                ↓
       (--local) runExecuteLoopInline(spec)
                ↓
       (default) httpSubmitExecuteLoop(spec)  ── JSON ──▶ server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker
                                                   ↓ (decode INTO THE SAME struct, then Validate + ApplyDefaults)
                                                startExecuteLoop(spec)
                                                   ↓ (persist as spec.json — SAME struct, fully-resolved)
                                                runWorker(spec)

Properties:
- One struct definition. JSON tags drive both wire format and disk persistence.
- Cobra binding is the only per-flag code (irreducible — cobra needs to know each flag).
- --local and server paths consume the same struct; behavior parity is structural, not asserted by tests.
- Adding a flag = (a) one struct field + (b) one cobra binding. Drop is impossible by construction — the JSON tag round-trips through wire and persistence.
- DispatchOptions is a SEPARATE small struct holding control-plane flags (--local, --json) that are NOT execution-spec fields and must NOT be persisted on disk.

CRITICAL CONTRACTS (per codex review)

1. Wire format for time.Duration: Go's default time.Duration JSON marshaling produces nanoseconds, but current code sends poll_interval as a string (agent_cmd.go:1969-1971). The unified spec MUST commit to one format — either custom Duration type with string MarshalJSON, or canonical numeric. Pick string (operator-readable spec.json wins) and add UnmarshalJSON tolerating both.

2. Validate + ApplyDefaults lifecycle:
   - parseExecuteLoopSpec returns spec + DispatchOptions, with cobra defaults applied
   - Spec.Validate() is called BEFORE HTTP submit (client-side guard)
   - server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes → calls Spec.ApplyDefaults() → Spec.Validate() BEFORE StartExecuteLoop (server cannot trust the wire)
   - GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter goes through THE SAME StartExecuteLoop entry point so Validate + ApplyDefaults are guaranteed
   - spec.json on disk is fully-resolved (no implicit defaults at runWorker time)

3. Forward-compat stance: ddx is single-repo, single-version client/server today. Add SpecVersion int field; server returns 400 with capability list if SpecVersion > supported. Newer-server-older-client: existing fields unchanged, new fields ignored on both sides (the current default). Make this explicit in the AC; do not rely on json's silent unknown-field tolerance as the only contract.

4. Backward-compat for old persisted specs: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ have legacy fields (e.g., min_tier/max_tier in older specs at .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T083934-f77c/spec.json:5-6). UnmarshalJSON must tolerate unknown fields when LOADING from disk (json.Decoder default behavior is fine here; do NOT enable DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads).

LOCATION

Likely cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go (a small focused package). Importable from both cli/cmd/ and cli/internal/server/ without import cycles. NOTE: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744) needs to know about this new package as an entry-root for the production-reachability checker.

NOT IN SCOPE

- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, ddx run, MCP agent dispatch, ddx work passthrough variants) — same smell may exist; covered by the sister audit bead ddx-fb290074.
- Reworking client→server transport (HTTP/JSON stays as-is).
- Changing validation logic itself — only ensuring it lives on the spec so both paths apply it.
- Renaming flags or reshaping CLI surface.
- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string — that is a picker semantics change, owned by ddx-9d55601f. Keep LabelFilter as string to keep ddx-9d55601f landing in parallel.

USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM (originally what filed this bead)

- Root cause: opaque_passthrough was dropped at the server's request struct. With OpaquePassthrough lost, the server-spawned worker validates the harness against its provider list (which rejects 'claude') instead of treating it as opaque-passthrough.
- The unification refactor solves this by construction — there's no second struct to omit OpaquePassthrough from. NOTE: OpaquePassthrough has TWO skip points to verify (cli/internal/server/workers.go:314-317 AND :752-773), not one.

This is the systematic application phase the user mandated: "go back through the codebase systematically applying it."

ABSORBS
- ddx-09d2990c (WIRE) — the 4 specific unwired functions become a subset of this sweep. Close WIRE as superseded by this bead at file time.
- Any clusters from /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md not already covered.
- Codex audit additions: jsonlFallbackForCollection (cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go:90), Store.breakStaleLock (lock.go:70), NewStoreWithBackend (store.go:119), WithSpokeHTTPClient (federation_spoke.go:103), Server.execStore (server.go:2028), GraphQL NewResolver (resolver.go:14).

EXPECTED WORKFLOW
1. Run ddx ac run --check production-reachability against the current worktree. Capture violation list.
2. For each violation, decide: WIRE (add to production code path) or DELETE (function is genuinely obsolete).
3. File one child bead per violation cluster (group by package or by originating story for traceability).
4. Drain the children. Each closes either via wiring PR or deletion PR.
5. Final pass: ddx ac run returns status=pass with no violations and no // wiring:pending annotations remaining (or all annotations cite still-open future beads).

DECISION RULE per violation
- If the symbol's originating bead's AC describes desired runtime behavior → WIRE.
- If the symbol was added speculatively or the design has moved on → DELETE; reopen the originating bead with obsolescence rationale, or close as obsolete.
- If neither is clear within 15 min of investigation → annotate // wiring:pending <follow-up-bead> and file the follow-up.

.ddx/executions/20260503T123048-397071dc/manifest.json:12:    "title": "checks: ship Go production-reachability check (library/checks/go-production-reachability)",
.ddx/executions/20260503T123048-397071dc/manifest.json:13:    "description": "Ship the Go-specific production-reachability check as a library resource conforming to the REACH-PROTO check protocol. NOT installed by default for arbitrary projects (Go-opinion would leak through ddx init). This project (ddx itself) opts in by adding .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml.

DESIGN
- Location: library/checks/go-production-reachability/ (alongside library/personas/, library/patterns/)
- Implementation: a small Go binary or shell script invoking golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode rooted at configurable entry packages.
- Default entry roots for ddx self-use: cli/cmd/..., cli/internal/server/graphql/..., cli/internal/server/http/...
- Logic:
  1. Read DIFF_BASE..DIFF_HEAD from env. Compute set of new Go symbols (functions, methods, types) added by the diff via AST parse of changed files.
  2. Run deadcode (or callgraph) rooted at the configured entry packages, EXCLUDING _test.go from roots and from reachability.
  3. For each new symbol not reachable from any entry root → violation { file, line, symbol, kind: "unreachable", detail: "no path from entry roots: <list>" }
  4. Recognize // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation as escape hatch for individual symbols. The check verifies the named bead exists and is open via local beads.jsonl read; otherwise the annotation is rejected.
  5. Write ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/production-reachability.json with the protocol-defined schema.
- Config: library/checks/go-production-reachability/check.yaml template that operators copy to .ddx/checks/ and customize entry_roots for their project.

DOGFOOD
- This project installs it: write .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml referencing the library check + cli's entry roots.
- The next ddx try on any bead touching cli/**/*.go will exercise the check.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Auto-install during ddx init (would leak Go-opinion). Operators opt in manually or via ddx install check (future, separate bead).
- Coverage-based "exercised via CLI" check (entered_via flavor). Static reachability is sufficient for the immediate problem.
- Python/TS/Rust analogues. Future contributions; protocol supports them.

DEP: REACH-PROTO (above)",
.ddx/executions/20260503T123048-397071dc/manifest.json:14:    "acceptance": "1. library/checks/go-production-reachability/ exists with: README.md (usage), check.yaml (template), implementation (binary or script).
2. Implementation reads DIFF_BASE/DIFF_HEAD/PROJECT_ROOT/EVIDENCE_DIR from env; computes new symbols from diff; runs deadcode-rooted reachability; writes result.json conforming to REACH-PROTO schema.
3. // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation parsed; bead existence + open-status verified against .ddx/beads.jsonl; invalid annotation = violation.
4. _test.go files excluded from both root selection and reachability calculation (so a unit-test-only reference does NOT satisfy the check).
5. Self-test: synthetic project with one unwired exported function returns status=block with that symbol in violations.
6. Self-test: same project with the function wired into cmd handler returns status=pass.
7. Self-test: same project with // wiring:pending annotation citing an open bead returns status=pass; annotation citing a closed/missing bead returns status=block.
8. This project installs it: .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml committed; ddx ac run on any current bead succeeds (or BLOCKs with the known unwired functions — see REACH-BACKFILL for resolution).
9. cd cli && go test green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
10. Evidence file at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/production-reachability.json shows the check ran and its findings.",

.ddx/executions/20260503T124415-1a86a675/prompt.md:102:+    "description": "Ship the Go-specific production-reachability check as a library resource conforming to the REACH-PROTO check protocol. NOT installed by default for arbitrary projects (Go-opinion would leak through ddx init). This project (ddx itself) opts in by adding .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml.

DESIGN
- Location: library/checks/go-production-reachability/ (alongside library/personas/, library/patterns/)
- Implementation: a small Go binary or shell script invoking golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode rooted at configurable entry packages.
- Default entry roots for ddx self-use: cli/cmd/..., cli/internal/server/graphql/..., cli/internal/server/http/...
- Logic:
  1. Read DIFF_BASE..DIFF_HEAD from env. Compute set of new Go symbols (functions, methods, types) added by the diff via AST parse of changed files.
  2. Run deadcode (or callgraph) rooted at the configured entry packages, EXCLUDING _test.go from roots and from reachability.
  3. For each new symbol not reachable from any entry root → violation { file, line, symbol, kind: "unreachable", detail: "no path from entry roots: <list>" }
  4. Recognize // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation as escape hatch for individual symbols. The check verifies the named bead exists and is open via local beads.jsonl read; otherwise the annotation is rejected.
  5. Write ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/production-reachability.json with the protocol-defined schema.
- Config: library/checks/go-production-reachability/check.yaml template that operators copy to .ddx/checks/ and customize entry_roots for their project.

DOGFOOD
- This project installs it: write .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml referencing the library check + cli's entry roots.
- The next ddx try on any bead touching cli/**/*.go will exercise the check.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Auto-install during ddx init (would leak Go-opinion). Operators opt in manually or via ddx install check (future, separate bead).
- Coverage-based "exercised via CLI" check (entered_via flavor). Static reachability is sufficient for the immediate problem.
- Python/TS/Rust analogues. Future contributions; protocol supports them.

DEP: REACH-PROTO (above)",

ABSORBS
- ddx-09d2990c (WIRE) — the 4 specific unwired functions become a subset of this sweep. Close WIRE as superseded by this bead at file time.
- Any clusters from /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md not already covered.
- Codex audit additions: jsonlFallbackForCollection (cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go:90), Store.breakStaleLock (lock.go:70), NewStoreWithBackend (store.go:119), WithSpokeHTTPClient (federation_spoke.go:103), Server.execStore (server.go:2028), GraphQL NewResolver (resolver.go:14).

EXPECTED WORKFLOW
1. Run ddx ac run --check production-reachability against the current worktree. Capture violation list.
2. For each violation, decide: WIRE (add to production code path) or DELETE (function is genuinely obsolete).
3. File one child bead per violation cluster (group by package or by originating story for traceability).
4. Drain the children. Each closes either via wiring PR or deletion PR.
5. Final pass: ddx ac run returns status=pass with no violations and no // wiring:pending annotations remaining (or all annotations cite still-open future beads).

DECISION RULE per violation
- If the symbol's originating bead's AC describes desired runtime behavior → WIRE.
- If the symbol was added speculatively or the design has moved on → DELETE; reopen the originating bead with obsolescence rationale, or close as obsolete.
- If neither is clear within 15 min of investigation → annotate // wiring:pending <follow-up-bead> and file the follow-up.

- Other languages (this project is Go-only at the check layer).

.ddx/executions/20260503T025809-45a220f7/manifest.json:13:    "description": "The bug at ddx-29058e2a (5 CLI flags silently dropped on the server path) is a symptom of a deeper architectural issue: the ExecuteLoopSpec is defined as parallel structs that must be hand-maintained in lockstep. Adding any flag requires editing 5+ places, and forgetting one is silent.

CURRENT STATE (the smell)

Each flag traverses 6 boundaries:

1. cobra extract: cmd.Flags().GetX("flag") → local var (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1573-1611)
2. positional param: passed to executeLoopWithServer / runExecuteLoopLocal (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1627, ~1941 signature)
3. loose JSON map: workerSpec map[string]interface{} (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:~1948)
4. server request struct: hand-maintained in handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (cli/internal/server/server.go:2246-2260)
5. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal: server.go:2289-2303 → cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49
6. runWorker per-field consumption: cli/internal/server/workers.go ~656

ALSO IN SCOPE — the GraphQL parallel path (per codex review):
- workerDispatchAdapter at cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:62-75, 123-137 has its own request struct AND its own StartExecuteLoop literal — same parallel-struct smell on a different transport. Unification must DELETE this too, otherwise the refactor fixes REST and leaves GraphQL re-broken.

Two of those (steps 4 and 5) are typed but maintained in parallel. The 5 dropped flags (opaque_passthrough, max_cost, request_timeout, min_power, max_power) fell out exactly there. PLUS FromRev is also dropped on the server path (cobra flag at agent_cmd.go:1560 + work.go:58, consumed locally at agent_cmd.go:1753 ExecuteBeadRuntime{FromRev: fromRev}, never reaches the server). The bug fix would be local — add fields in 5 places — but the ROOT CAUSE recurs the next time a flag is added.

DESIGN — single canonical ExecuteLoopSpec struct

Define one struct, used at every layer:

  cobra RunE → parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions)
                ↓
       (--local) runExecuteLoopInline(spec)
                ↓
       (default) httpSubmitExecuteLoop(spec)  ── JSON ──▶ server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker
                                                   ↓ (decode INTO THE SAME struct, then Validate + ApplyDefaults)
                                                startExecuteLoop(spec)
                                                   ↓ (persist as spec.json — SAME struct, fully-resolved)
                                                runWorker(spec)

Properties:
- One struct definition. JSON tags drive both wire format and disk persistence.
- Cobra binding is the only per-flag code (irreducible — cobra needs to know each flag).
- --local and server paths consume the same struct; behavior parity is structural, not asserted by tests.
- Adding a flag = (a) one struct field + (b) one cobra binding. Drop is impossible by construction — the JSON tag round-trips through wire and persistence.
- DispatchOptions is a SEPARATE small struct holding control-plane flags (--local, --json) that are NOT execution-spec fields and must NOT be persisted on disk.

CRITICAL CONTRACTS (per codex review)

1. Wire format for time.Duration: Go's default time.Duration JSON marshaling produces nanoseconds, but current code sends poll_interval as a string (agent_cmd.go:1969-1971). The unified spec MUST commit to one format — either custom Duration type with string MarshalJSON, or canonical numeric. Pick string (operator-readable spec.json wins) and add UnmarshalJSON tolerating both.

2. Validate + ApplyDefaults lifecycle:
   - parseExecuteLoopSpec returns spec + DispatchOptions, with cobra defaults applied
   - Spec.Validate() is called BEFORE HTTP submit (client-side guard)
   - server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes → calls Spec.ApplyDefaults() → Spec.Validate() BEFORE StartExecuteLoop (server cannot trust the wire)
   - GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter goes through THE SAME StartExecuteLoop entry point so Validate + ApplyDefaults are guaranteed
   - spec.json on disk is fully-resolved (no implicit defaults at runWorker time)

3. Forward-compat stance: ddx is single-repo, single-version client/server today. Add SpecVersion int field; server returns 400 with capability list if SpecVersion > supported. Newer-server-older-client: existing fields unchanged, new fields ignored on both sides (the current default). Make this explicit in the AC; do not rely on json's silent unknown-field tolerance as the only contract.

4. Backward-compat for old persisted specs: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ have legacy fields (e.g., min_tier/max_tier in older specs at .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T083934-f77c/spec.json:5-6). UnmarshalJSON must tolerate unknown fields when LOADING from disk (json.Decoder default behavior is fine here; do NOT enable DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads).

LOCATION

Likely cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go (a small focused package). Importable from both cli/cmd/ and cli/internal/server/ without import cycles. NOTE: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744) needs to know about this new package as an entry-root for the production-reachability checker.

NOT IN SCOPE

- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, ddx run, MCP agent dispatch, ddx work passthrough variants) — same smell may exist; covered by the sister audit bead ddx-fb290074.
- Reworking client→server transport (HTTP/JSON stays as-is).
- Changing validation logic itself — only ensuring it lives on the spec so both paths apply it.
- Renaming flags or reshaping CLI surface.
- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string — that is a picker semantics change, owned by ddx-9d55601f. Keep LabelFilter as string to keep ddx-9d55601f landing in parallel.

USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM (originally what filed this bead)
- ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server.
- Root cause: opaque_passthrough was dropped at the server's request struct. With OpaquePassthrough lost, the server-spawned worker validates the harness against its provider list (which rejects 'claude') instead of treating it as opaque-passthrough.
- The unification refactor solves this by construction — there's no second struct to omit OpaquePassthrough from. NOTE: OpaquePassthrough has TWO skip points to verify (cli/internal/server/workers.go:314-317 AND :752-773), not one.

ABSORBS THE NARROW FIX
The 5 originally-listed dropped flags + FromRev all become fields on the unified spec. They cannot be dropped after this refactor. The refactor IS the fix.

SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS

- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): touches execute_bead_loop.go's Run loop. Should land BEFORE this OR be tightly coordinated. If unification lands first, dc157075 redoes defaulting + persistence. Recommend dc157075 first.
- ddx-9d55601f (picker priority bug): does NOT block on this. Keep LabelFilter type stable. Picker fix lands in parallel.
- ddx-a946c744 (REACH-PROTO): needs to know about new cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ package as entry root.",

.ddx/executions/20260503T030952-1063db26/prompt.md:3:    <title>checks: ship Go production-reachability check (library/checks/go-production-reachability)</title>
.ddx/executions/20260503T030952-1063db26/prompt.md:5:Ship the Go-specific production-reachability check as a library resource conforming to the REACH-PROTO check protocol. NOT installed by default for arbitrary projects (Go-opinion would leak through ddx init). This project (ddx itself) opts in by adding .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml.

DESIGN
- Location: library/checks/go-production-reachability/ (alongside library/personas/, library/patterns/)
- Implementation: a small Go binary or shell script invoking golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode rooted at configurable entry packages.
- Default entry roots for ddx self-use: cli/cmd/..., cli/internal/server/graphql/..., cli/internal/server/http/...
- Logic:
  1. Read DIFF_BASE..DIFF_HEAD from env. Compute set of new Go symbols (functions, methods, types) added by the diff via AST parse of changed files.
  2. Run deadcode (or callgraph) rooted at the configured entry packages, EXCLUDING _test.go from roots and from reachability.
  3. For each new symbol not reachable from any entry root → violation { file, line, symbol, kind: "unreachable", detail: "no path from entry roots: <list>" }
  4. Recognize // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation as escape hatch for individual symbols. The check verifies the named bead exists and is open via local beads.jsonl read; otherwise the annotation is rejected.
  5. Write ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/production-reachability.json with the protocol-defined schema.
- Config: library/checks/go-production-reachability/check.yaml template that operators copy to .ddx/checks/ and customize entry_roots for their project.

DOGFOOD
- This project installs it: write .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml referencing the library check + cli's entry roots.
- The next ddx try on any bead touching cli/**/*.go will exercise the check.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Auto-install during ddx init (would leak Go-opinion). Operators opt in manually or via ddx install check (future, separate bead).
- Coverage-based "exercised via CLI" check (entered_via flavor). Static reachability is sufficient for the immediate problem.
- Python/TS/Rust analogues. Future contributions; protocol supports them.

DEP: REACH-PROTO (above)
.ddx/executions/20260503T030952-1063db26/prompt.md:8:1. library/checks/go-production-reachability/ exists with: README.md (usage), check.yaml (template), implementation (binary or script).
2. Implementation reads DIFF_BASE/DIFF_HEAD/PROJECT_ROOT/EVIDENCE_DIR from env; computes new symbols from diff; runs deadcode-rooted reachability; writes result.json conforming to REACH-PROTO schema.
3. // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation parsed; bead existence + open-status verified against .ddx/beads.jsonl; invalid annotation = violation.
4. _test.go files excluded from both root selection and reachability calculation (so a unit-test-only reference does NOT satisfy the check).
5. Self-test: synthetic project with one unwired exported function returns status=block with that symbol in violations.
6. Self-test: same project with the function wired into cmd handler returns status=pass.
7. Self-test: same project with // wiring:pending annotation citing an open bead returns status=pass; annotation citing a closed/missing bead returns status=block.
8. This project installs it: .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml committed; ddx ac run on any current bead succeeds (or BLOCKs with the known unwired functions — see REACH-BACKFILL for resolution).
9. cd cli && go test green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
10. Evidence file at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/production-reachability.json shows the check ran and its findings.
.ddx/executions/20260503T025809-45a220f7/prompt.md:5:The bug at ddx-29058e2a (5 CLI flags silently dropped on the server path) is a symptom of a deeper architectural issue: the ExecuteLoopSpec is defined as parallel structs that must be hand-maintained in lockstep. Adding any flag requires editing 5+ places, and forgetting one is silent.

CURRENT STATE (the smell)

Each flag traverses 6 boundaries:

1. cobra extract: cmd.Flags().GetX("flag") → local var (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1573-1611)
2. positional param: passed to executeLoopWithServer / runExecuteLoopLocal (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1627, ~1941 signature)
3. loose JSON map: workerSpec map[string]interface{} (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:~1948)
4. server request struct: hand-maintained in handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (cli/internal/server/server.go:2246-2260)
5. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal: server.go:2289-2303 → cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49
6. runWorker per-field consumption: cli/internal/server/workers.go ~656

ALSO IN SCOPE — the GraphQL parallel path (per codex review):
- workerDispatchAdapter at cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:62-75, 123-137 has its own request struct AND its own StartExecuteLoop literal — same parallel-struct smell on a different transport. Unification must DELETE this too, otherwise the refactor fixes REST and leaves GraphQL re-broken.

Two of those (steps 4 and 5) are typed but maintained in parallel. The 5 dropped flags (opaque_passthrough, max_cost, request_timeout, min_power, max_power) fell out exactly there. PLUS FromRev is also dropped on the server path (cobra flag at agent_cmd.go:1560 + work.go:58, consumed locally at agent_cmd.go:1753 ExecuteBeadRuntime{FromRev: fromRev}, never reaches the server). The bug fix would be local — add fields in 5 places — but the ROOT CAUSE recurs the next time a flag is added.

DESIGN — single canonical ExecuteLoopSpec struct

Define one struct, used at every layer:

  cobra RunE → parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions)
                ↓
       (--local) runExecuteLoopInline(spec)
                ↓
       (default) httpSubmitExecuteLoop(spec)  ── JSON ──▶ server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker
                                                   ↓ (decode INTO THE SAME struct, then Validate + ApplyDefaults)
                                                startExecuteLoop(spec)
                                                   ↓ (persist as spec.json — SAME struct, fully-resolved)
                                                runWorker(spec)

Properties:
- One struct definition. JSON tags drive both wire format and disk persistence.
- Cobra binding is the only per-flag code (irreducible — cobra needs to know each flag).
- --local and server paths consume the same struct; behavior parity is structural, not asserted by tests.
- Adding a flag = (a) one struct field + (b) one cobra binding. Drop is impossible by construction — the JSON tag round-trips through wire and persistence.
- DispatchOptions is a SEPARATE small struct holding control-plane flags (--local, --json) that are NOT execution-spec fields and must NOT be persisted on disk.

CRITICAL CONTRACTS (per codex review)

1. Wire format for time.Duration: Go's default time.Duration JSON marshaling produces nanoseconds, but current code sends poll_interval as a string (agent_cmd.go:1969-1971). The unified spec MUST commit to one format — either custom Duration type with string MarshalJSON, or canonical numeric. Pick string (operator-readable spec.json wins) and add UnmarshalJSON tolerating both.

2. Validate + ApplyDefaults lifecycle:
   - parseExecuteLoopSpec returns spec + DispatchOptions, with cobra defaults applied
   - Spec.Validate() is called BEFORE HTTP submit (client-side guard)
   - server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes → calls Spec.ApplyDefaults() → Spec.Validate() BEFORE StartExecuteLoop (server cannot trust the wire)
   - GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter goes through THE SAME StartExecuteLoop entry point so Validate + ApplyDefaults are guaranteed
   - spec.json on disk is fully-resolved (no implicit defaults at runWorker time)

3. Forward-compat stance: ddx is single-repo, single-version client/server today. Add SpecVersion int field; server returns 400 with capability list if SpecVersion > supported. Newer-server-older-client: existing fields unchanged, new fields ignored on both sides (the current default). Make this explicit in the AC; do not rely on json's silent unknown-field tolerance as the only contract.

4. Backward-compat for old persisted specs: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ have legacy fields (e.g., min_tier/max_tier in older specs at .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T083934-f77c/spec.json:5-6). UnmarshalJSON must tolerate unknown fields when LOADING from disk (json.Decoder default behavior is fine here; do NOT enable DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads).

LOCATION

Likely cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go (a small focused package). Importable from both cli/cmd/ and cli/internal/server/ without import cycles. NOTE: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744) needs to know about this new package as an entry-root for the production-reachability checker.

NOT IN SCOPE

- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, ddx run, MCP agent dispatch, ddx work passthrough variants) — same smell may exist; covered by the sister audit bead ddx-fb290074.
- Reworking client→server transport (HTTP/JSON stays as-is).
- Changing validation logic itself — only ensuring it lives on the spec so both paths apply it.
- Renaming flags or reshaping CLI surface.
- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string — that is a picker semantics change, owned by ddx-9d55601f. Keep LabelFilter as string to keep ddx-9d55601f landing in parallel.

USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM (originally what filed this bead)
- ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server.
- Root cause: opaque_passthrough was dropped at the server's request struct. With OpaquePassthrough lost, the server-spawned worker validates the harness against its provider list (which rejects 'claude') instead of treating it as opaque-passthrough.
- The unification refactor solves this by construction — there's no second struct to omit OpaquePassthrough from. NOTE: OpaquePassthrough has TWO skip points to verify (cli/internal/server/workers.go:314-317 AND :752-773), not one.

ABSORBS THE NARROW FIX
The 5 originally-listed dropped flags + FromRev all become fields on the unified spec. They cannot be dropped after this refactor. The refactor IS the fix.

SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS

- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): touches execute_bead_loop.go's Run loop. Should land BEFORE this OR be tightly coordinated. If unification lands first, dc157075 redoes defaulting + persistence. Recommend dc157075 first.
- ddx-9d55601f (picker priority bug): does NOT block on this. Keep LabelFilter type stable. Picker fix lands in parallel.
- ddx-a946c744 (REACH-PROTO): needs to know about new cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ package as entry root.

.ddx/executions/20260503T025720-bb59ccca/manifest.json:12:    "title": "ddx try: pre-merge production-reachability check protocol + runner",
.ddx/executions/20260503T025720-bb59ccca/manifest.json:13:    "description": "Add a language-agnostic check protocol to ddx so projects can declare mechanical pre-merge gates that ddx try evaluates before any worktree is merged back. Solves the recurring "implemented but not wired" failure mode at the platform layer rather than per-project.

CONTEXT
- Audit at /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md surfaced 4 functions with tests but no production caller; reviewer didn't catch it because the diff "looked right" and tests passed.
- ac_structural_check.go (cli/internal/agent/) is the existing seed: parses claims from AC prose for test_name/file_deleted/field_removed. It can't catch the unwired case because a unit test naming the function satisfies the claim vacuously.
- Prior plan considered embedding Go-specific call-graph analysis directly in cli/internal/agent/. Rejected as a boundary violation (CLAUDE.md: "DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions"). Each language ecosystem has its own dead-code tooling (Go: deadcode/callgraph; Python: vulture; TS: ts-prune/knip; Rust: cargo-udeps).

DESIGN — protocol (declarative, plugin-based)
- Discovery: .ddx/checks/*.yaml each declares one check: { name, command, when: pre_merge, applies_to: { paths: [globs] | always, labels: [...] } }
- Inputs to check command (env vars): BEAD_ID, DIFF_BASE, DIFF_HEAD, PROJECT_ROOT, EVIDENCE_DIR, RUN_ID
- Output contract: check writes ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json with schema { status: pass|block|error, message: string, violations: [{file, line, symbol, kind, detail}] }
- Exit code 0 = ran-to-completion (status field is authoritative); non-zero = check itself errored (treated as BLOCK with status=error)
- Hook point: pre_merge — runs in the parent (after worker's worktree work is done, BEFORE the merge-back into integration branch). BLOCK aborts the merge; the worktree stays preserved per existing conventions for human resolution.
- Bypass: bead-level annotation checks_bypass: [{name: <check>, reason: <text>, bead: <bead-id>}] recorded in events. No CLI flag — bypass must be tracked.
- Standalone: ddx ac run <bead-id> [--check <name>] runs the same checks locally so devs can iterate without spawning a full try attempt.

WHY pre_merge (NOT pre_close in the worker)
- User directive: "We can't afford to merge this kind of dead code." Worker-side checks could be bypassed by a worker that ignores BLOCK; parent-side gate cannot.
- Worker still gets feedback via ddx ac run for local iteration.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Multiple lifecycle hook points beyond pre_merge (add later if needed).
- Check dependencies / ordering (independent + parallel until proven insufficient).
- Plugin manifests, capability flags, version negotiation.
- Auto-discovery beyond .ddx/checks/*.yaml.
- Retry / timeout policy (project owns; checks should be fast).
- Any specific language check (separate beads).

OUT OF SCOPE: REACH-GO (the Go-specific deadcode-based check) and REACH-BACKFILL (the systematic application across cli/) are separate beads that depend on this one.

FILES (anticipated)
- cli/internal/checks/protocol.go — config schema + JSON I/O types
- cli/internal/checks/loader.go — read .ddx/checks/*.yaml
- cli/internal/checks/runner.go — subprocess invocation, env injection, output parsing, evidence dir mgmt
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go — wire pre_merge call before merge step; BLOCK aborts merge
- cli/cmd/ac.go — ddx ac run subcommand
- cli/internal/checks/*_test.go — table-driven tests for protocol contract",

  QuorumOptions: ~5 occurrences across compare_adapter.go,

these structs at the four CLI entry points and a portion of
agent-package tests. The structs are still load-bearing for:

    runVirtualFn, RunAgent, runScriptFn, runClaudeWithFallbackFn,

  - The `runner.Run(RunOptions{...})` self-call inside
    runner.go::TestProviderConnectivity (line 976).
  - The `r.Run(RunOptions{...})` call inside grade.go::169.

================================================================

================================================================

Stage 1's analogous cleanup (ExecuteBeadLoopOptions) was 18 files /
~123 net lines. Stage 2's RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions
cleanup is ~3-4× larger by raw reference count, and ~2× larger
structurally because (a) RunOptions is used as the central internal
data carrier inside compare_adapter.go's full Run / RunCompare /
RunQuorum pipelines (not just at the dispatch entry), and (b)
external callers in three different packages (cmd/, internal/exec/,

construction wired in, which means the `agent.RunViaService` /

the per-bead size budget defined in SD-024 §"Per-bead size budget"
(target <500 LoC, <20 minutes, <$10 spend).

NO CHANGES — bead preconditions not satisfied; scope exceeds single-window budget.

## What is done

- Read the bead, SD-024, TD-024, runtimelint analyzer, and current state of
  cli/internal/agent/types.go and runner.go.
- Surveyed actual caller surface in the worktree (HEAD = 562f293c).

## Why no commit was made

The bead description states explicit dependencies:

  "Depends on bead 16 (Stage 1 cleanup) AND beads 17-21 (every Stage 2
   caller migrated)."

The bead's own reopen note (2026-04-26) confirms those dependencies are
NOT satisfied:

  "type RunOptions still exists at cli/internal/agent/types.go:36;
   QuorumOptions at :81; CompareOptions at :171. runtimelint reports
   ~80 violations across the tree using these types. Closure was fake
   (already_satisfied or premature). The retirement was NOT actually
   performed — only ExecuteBeadLoopOptions (B16) and ExecuteBeadOptions
   (B28) shipped real type-deletion commits."

So B17-B21 (the per-caller Stage 2 migration beads) were never done.
Today's measured surface, from this worktree:

  rg "RunOptions|CompareOptions|QuorumOptions" cli/  →  188 references
                                                        across 48 files
  rg "RunOptions\{|CompareOptions\{|QuorumOptions\{" cli/
                                                     →  ~50 composite literals
                                                        (~13 production,
                                                         ~31 in tests,
                                                         ~5 in lint testdata,
                                                         rest in agent
                                                         package internals)

Production constructor sites:
  cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1462
  cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go:61, 261, 414, 685
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:647
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:531
  cli/internal/agent/grade.go:169
  cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:82
  cli/internal/exec/store.go:201
  cli/internal/server/server.go:1913, 3786

Internal-implementation surface (beyond the two files the bead names as
"in-scope"): every dispatch helper in the agent package threads
RunOptions through its signature —

  agent_runner.go, agent_runner_service.go, claude_stream.go,
  compare_adapter.go, execute_bead.go, routing_metrics.go, runner.go,
  script.go, service_run.go, session_index.go, virtual.go

plus BuildArgs, RunAgent, runVirtualFn, runScriptFn,
runClaudeWithFallbackFn, finalizeClaudeResult, processResult, and
TestProviderConnectivity all take RunOptions or fields from it.
CompareOptions and QuorumOptions both EMBED RunOptions, so deleting
RunOptions also forces a redesign of compare_adapter.go's option flow.

## What is blocking

The bead is framed as a one-shot type-deletion ("delete the structs,
rename RunWithConfig → Run"), but the AC requires `go build` and
`go test` to pass tree-wide. To pass those gates the bead has to
also:

1. Replace every internal RunOptions parameter (agent package
   helpers) with an AgentRunRuntime + ResolvedConfig pair, OR keep
   them on a non-RunOptions internal type.
2. Migrate all ~13 production caller sites to RunWithConfig
   (renamed Run), including building a config.ResolvedConfig at each
   site (CLI, server, exec store, grade, compare_adapter,
   execute_bead, execute_bead_review, service_run).
3. Migrate or delete the CompareOptions/QuorumOptions surface
   (compare_adapter.go is heavily built around it; this is essentially
   a rewrite of the comparison/quorum dispatcher, not a rename).
4. Migrate ~31 test composite literals across agent_test.go,
   claude_stream_test.go, prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go,
   session_index_test.go, models_test.go, virtual_test.go,
   plus runtime tests.
5. Update or remove evidencelint analyzer entries for RunOptions
   and the runtimelint testdata (which deliberately uses the legacy
   types as fixtures).

This is the SD-024 §"Migrate one production caller per bead" / §"Migrate
test files in groups" work — the ~5 beads (B17–B21) the bead description
points at as completed dependencies. Doing all of that here in a single
execute-bead window violates the SD's own per-bead size budget
("<500 LoC of net change, <20 minutes of agent time, under $10 of model
spend") by an order of magnitude. The realistic LoC delta is in the
1500–2500 range across 30+ files.

## What a follow-up attempt would need

Reopen B17–B21 (or their successor beads) with concrete per-file scopes
before re-queuing this bead. Suggested split:

- B17: migrate cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go RunViaService caller +
       cli/internal/server/server.go (2 sites). Add a
       config.ResolvedConfig handoff at each site.
- B18: migrate cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go +
       execute_bead_review.go + service_run.go (3 sites; agent-package
       internal callers).
- B19: migrate cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go and
       cli/internal/agent/grade.go — biggest surface; redesigns
       CompareOptions/QuorumOptions on top of AgentRunRuntime
       (likely promotes Harnesses/Strategy/Threshold/ArmModels/
       ArmLabels/Sandbox/KeepSandbox/PostRun onto a new
       CompareRuntime/QuorumRuntime).
- B20: migrate cli/internal/exec/store.go + types.go.
- B21: migrate the ~31 test composite literals; update
       evidencelint analyzer + testdata.

Once those land green, this bead (B22) becomes the trivially-correct
final cleanup — delete the three types, rename RunWithConfig → Run,
update the runtimelint testdata that intentionally uses the legacy
names, and confirm runtimelint reports zero violations on the live
tree.

Recommendation: do NOT escalate this bead to a stronger model yet.
The blocker is the missing prior beads, not model capability. A
stronger model attempting the full ~2000-LoC migration in one
worker window would produce churn that is hard to review and likely
breaks one of the three production dispatch sites in a non-obvious
way (the historical bug class SD-024 was created to prevent).
rationale: Bead: ddx-ade5ebb3 — agent: retire RunOptions + CompareOptions + QuorumOptions + legacy Run

rationale: no_changes rationale — ddx-ade5ebb3 (Stage 2 retire RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions + legacy Run)

WHAT IS DONE
- Surveyed full call graph for the three doomed types and the legacy Run method.
- Confirmed bead notes: dependencies (beads 17–21) ARE all closed, but they migrated
  only the public CLI command paths (`ddx agent run`, `ddx agent compare`,
  `ddx agent quorum`) — not the internal agent-package plumbing nor the
  external server/exec callers.

WHAT IS BLOCKING
The bead's stated in-scope is two files (cli/internal/agent/types.go,
cli/internal/agent/runner.go), but the AC requires `cd cli && go build ./...`
and `cd cli && go test ./...` to pass after the three exported types and the
legacy Run method are removed. Reality of references that must move
together (ripgrep counts):

A. agent.RunOptions / CompareOptions / QuorumOptions — 88 references in
   non-testdata Go files outside the lint testdata trees. Of these:

   Internal to cli/internal/agent/ (cannot be deleted without parallel rewrite):
   - virtual.go (runVirtualFn signature + builder)
   - script.go (runScriptFn signature)
   - claude_stream.go (4 helpers: resolveClaudeProgressLogDir,
     runClaudeStreamingFn, runClaudeWithFallbackFn, finalizeClaudeResult)
   - agent_runner.go (RunAgent), agent_runner_service.go (runAgentViaService)
   - models.go (BenchmarkArmsToCompare → CompareOptions)
   - grade.go (r.Run(RunOptions{...}))
   - service_run.go (RunViaService, RunViaServiceWith, runFixtureHarnessViaRunner — public exported entry points)
   - session_index.go (SessionIndexEntryFromResult — exported)
   - routing_metrics.go (recordRoutingOutcome)
   - compare_adapter.go (~25 references — RunFunc, RunCompareWith, RunCompareViaService, RunCompareWithAgent, defaultResolvePromptForCompare, runCompareArmWith, RunQuorumWith, RunQuorumViaService, RunQuorumWithAgent, RunBenchmarkWith, plus the existing RunCompareWithConfigViaService/RunQuorumWithConfig adapters that still build CompareOptions/QuorumOptions internally)
   - execute_bead.go (AgentRunner interface signature + dispatchAgentRun + the runOpts builder around line 647)
   - execute_bead_review.go (DefaultBeadReviewer.dispatchReviewRun + the AgentRunner field at line 425)
   - runner.go itself (resolveHarness, resolvePrompt, resolveModel, resolveTimeout, resolveWallClock, BuildArgs, recordRoutingOutcome, TestProviderConnectivity, plus the legacy Run body and the Run alias for AgentRunner satisfaction)

   External callers (cli/internal/server, cli/internal/exec, cli/cmd):
   - cli/internal/server/server.go:1913 + :3786 (handleAgentRun + workers handler — both build agent.RunOptions{} for RunViaService)
   - cli/internal/exec/types.go:11 (AgentRunner interface signature uses agent.RunOptions)
   - cli/internal/exec/store.go:201 (builds agent.RunOptions for s.AgentRunner.Run)
   - cli/cmd/exec.go:98 (serviceExecAgentRunner.Run wraps RunViaService)
   - cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1462 (RunViaService call building RunOptions)

   Test mocks that implement AgentRunner (must update their Run signature
   in lockstep with the interface change):
   - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_runtime_test.go (capturingAgentRunner)
   - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go (reviewRunnerStub)
   - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_evidence_test.go (countingRunner)
   - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_env_isolation_test.go (envIsoRunner)
   - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_e2e_test.go (gateTestAgentRunner, rationaleTestRunner)
   - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_artifacts_test.go (artifactTestAgentRunner)
   - cli/internal/agent/executions_mirror_test.go (artifactTestAgentRunner usage)
   - cli/internal/exec/store_test.go (mockAgentRunner)
   - cli/cmd/exec_acceptance_test.go (mockAgentRunnerCmd)
   - cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go (fakeAgentRunner, modelPassthroughCapture — also captures `last agent.RunOptions`)
   - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go (run-closure for RunCompareWith on line 584)
   - Plus the BuildArgs(h, RunOptions{...}, "") test fixtures across agent_test.go (~16 sites) — these use the struct literal form, not interface satisfaction, so they migrate trivially via field-by-field replacement, but they still count.

B. Legacy Runner.Run satisfies agent.AgentRunner interface today. Several
   tests pass *Runner directly into ExecuteBeadRuntime{AgentRunner: runner}
   (integration_helper_test.go:184, execute_bead_runtime_test.go:56,
   execute_bead_routing_evidence_test.go:35/85/116,
   execute_bead_e2e_test.go:358/403). Removing the legacy Run AND renaming
   RunWithConfig → Run breaks this satisfaction unless either:
     (a) The agent.AgentRunner interface itself is migrated to take
         (ctx, ResolvedConfig, AgentRunRuntime) — cascades to every mock
         listed in (A) above, AND to cli/internal/exec/types.go's
         AgentRunner because cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:152 assigns one to
         the other (Go's structural interface satisfaction).
     (b) An adapter type is introduced (e.g., agent.RunnerAsAgentRunner)
         and every test wiring rewritten — ~10 test sites.

WHY THIS IS A NO_CHANGES, NOT A BAD COMMIT
Two prior attempts on this bead were "fake-closed" (per the bead notes) —
likely because they tried to satisfy the AC verifier via the loophole that
the field_removed regex `\bX\s+[\*\[\]\w.]+` does not match
`type X = Y` aliases or `(opts X)` parameter lists. A type-alias
relocation (defining the structs in cli/internal/agentopts/ and aliasing
them back into the agent package) WOULD satisfy the regex check and the
manual rg over types.go alone, while keeping every external caller
working. But that is the same loophole that the bead author explicitly
called out and reopened twice for. The bead notes say: "Worker must
actually delete the three type definitions and migrate every call site."
Doing that requires the multi-file refactor described above.

That refactor is well-defined but is at least 30 files, ~150 hunks of
edits, and depends on a coordinated public-API redesign of:
  - agent.RunViaService / RunViaServiceWith (signature change)
  - agent.AgentRunner interface (signature change, cascades to exec.AgentRunner)
  - cli/internal/exec.AgentRunner interface (signature change)
  - The Runner.Run / Runner.RunWithConfig swap with a new private
    runInternal(opts) for internal callers (grade.go, service_run.go,
    runner.go itself, execute_bead_review.go) so they don't build a fake
    ResolvedConfig just to dispatch.

That is genuinely epic-sized — bigger than the four Stage-2 dependency
beads put together — and committing it half-done in this single worktree
risks leaving the build red on main.

WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT WOULD NEED
Either:

(1) Re-decompose this bead into ≥5 smaller beads that can each land green:
    - 24a "agent: introduce private runInternal + new agent.AgentRunner signature with RunArgs adapter type"
    - 24b "exec: migrate AgentRunner interface to (ctx, rcfg, runtime) signature; update store + cmd/exec wrapper"
    - 24c "server: migrate handleAgentRun + workers handler to agent.RunViaServiceWithConfig"
    - 24d "agent: migrate internal helpers (virtual, script, claude_stream, agent_runner, models, grade, service_run, session_index, routing_metrics, compare_adapter) off RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions struct names"
    - 24e (this bead) "agent: delete the three exported type definitions + delete legacy Runner.Run + rename RunWithConfig → Run"
    Each prerequisite bead has a clear gate-verifiable AC and the final
    bead becomes a 2-file mechanical removal as originally scoped.

(2) Or: explicitly authorize this bead to expand its scope to the full
    30-file refactor and accept that it will land as one large PR. The
    bead description's "In-scope" lines and the AgentRunner interface
    discussion both need updating before a worker tries again.

The structural blocker is that the bead is written as a 2-file cleanup
but the AC + dependencies (which were not actually completed) require an
epic-scale rewrite. A stronger model will not solve that mismatch — it
needs either re-decomposition or an explicit scope-expansion ack.
